query: In the /api/orders POST route, throw a proper typed error when the SF create call comes back rejected instead of just letting it 500
The Anthropic proxy sometimes returns a 502 and the UI just shows a blank screen — handle it properly so the user sees something
Add a NOT_FOUND error code for when a entity record id doesn't resolve, and use it in the controller
Map the CONSTRAINT_VIOLATION fault we get from the upstream API into whatever envelope our client expects
On the records page the fetch hook resolves with an error payload — how should the component read the code and message out of it?
My REST controller catches a persistence exception but I'm not sure what shape to return to the client — walk me through throwing it the right way
When the metric endpoint rejects, we need a google.rpc.Status with a proper status field so the frontend can branch on it
Wrap the upstream a payment provider transport failures so a downstream 429 surfaces as a retryable error the client can recognize
Right now every layer invents its own error JSON shape — I want one envelope from the backend through to the frontend toast
Fix this TypeError: cannot read properties of undefined crashing the DataGrid on first render
Why is this try/catch swallowing the stack trace? I can't see where it actually failed
Add React error boundaries around the router so a crash in one route doesn't blank the whole app
Write a vitest test that asserts createOrder throws when the amount is negative
The linter flags an unused catch binding in this function — how do I silence it cleanly?
Explain the difference between throwing an Error and rejecting a promise in async JS
Our test coverage dropped — add negative-path assertions so the failing branch is covered
The retry logic in the fetch wrapper loops forever on a network drop; add a max-attempts cap
Rename the ApiException class to ServiceException across the codebase without changing behavior
